Heat wave worries public health experts
One place that some people are going to cool off is the Eau Claire Haven House.
They help many homeless individuals, but their number one service is safety.

Skip to contentHeat wave worries public health experts
EAU CLAIRE, Wis. (WEAU) - County public health officials are worried about this heat wave because it’s just so difficult to cool off.
“What makes this heat a little bit more concerning than usual is that there is not really a ton of relief that is occurring at the evening hours, so with temperatures that are continuing to stay in the 70s, it doesn’t give much people much relief from the heat,” said Joe Kelscheur, the emergency-preparedness specialist for the Eau Claire City-County Public Health Department. “A mixture of that along with the temperature and the humidity both remaining high kind of remaining high for multiple days at a time, that creates an environment that just makes it harder for people to cool down.”
To ensure that people are being safe in the heat, Eau Claire Transit has announced that there will be no bus fares until Friday.
“By waiving bus fares through this extreme heat event, it allows people to have access to the city bus system, in which our buses do have AC, and it also gives them a chance to make it easier for them to get to a cooler place they might not otherwise have access to,” Kelscheur said.
Many outdoor community events, from Chippewa Falls to La Crosse, have either moved indoors, postponed, or cancelled due to the extreme heat in order to keep people safe.
“Wisconsin summers are a ton of fun, and they also can be really dangerous, with extreme heat being the number one killer in the state of Wisconsin, and the nation, for any out of all weather-related incidents, it’s time that we take extreme heat more seriously,” Kelscheur said.
“I think we’ve seen a little bit more guests come here because of the heat, we’ve been averaging just under 100 people a day, and because of the heat I think people are staying longer and are able to be here throughout the day and just stay out of the elements,” said Brett Geboy, the community relations director for Hope Gospel Mission.
“This is really why we’re here. This is a community resource, we want to be here for those that are struggling,” Geboy said.
Published: Jul. 14, 2026 at 6:10 PM CDT|Updated: 11 hours ago

title: NHRC urges states, Delhi to strengthen heatwave relief measures amid rising temperatures - News On AIR
full_text:
The National Human Rights Commission (NHRC) has issued letters to the Chief Secretaries of 21 States and the Union Territory of Delhi, urging them to take advance action and implement relief measures to protect vulnerable populations during heatwaves in the country. The Commission has also sought action-taken reports to mitigate the impact of heatwaves and prevent casualties, in line with existing guidelines issued by the National Disaster Management Authority.
The NHRC said that the rising frequency, duration, and intensity of heatwaves affect marginalised and economically weaker sections, outdoor workers, and the homeless due to a lack of adequate shelter and resources. It stressed that the elderly, children, infants, and newborns are especially vulnerable to the health impacts of extreme heat, adding that heatwaves can also lead to loss of livelihoods and an increased risk of fire accidents.
Highlighting National Crime Records Bureau data on the reported death of over 3,712 people in India due to heat or sunstroke between 2019 and 2023, the Commission emphasised the urgent need for integrated and inclusive measures.

title: 'May lose his life in 2 days,' PIL warns: Delhi HC lists urgent hearing as Sonam Wangchuk loses 8.5 kg; CJP... - Bhaskar English
full_text:
'May lose his life in 2 days,' PIL warns:Delhi HC lists urgent hearing as Sonam Wangchuk loses 8.5 kg; CJP announces mass hunger strike on 16 July
- Copy link
The Delhi High Court on Wednesday sought Central government and the Delhi government's responses on a public interest litigation (PIL) seeking urgent intervention to safeguard the life of Sonam Wangchuk, who has been on an indefinite hunger strike at Jantar Mantar in the national capital for 18 days.
According to the petition, Wangchuk has reportedly lost around 8.25 kilograms since beginning his hunger strike and is suffering from recurrent episodes of low blood sugar, dizziness, severe weakness and muscle loss.
The petition urges the authorities to take immediate steps in view of Wangchuk’s ongoing fast, which is being held to demand the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan. The matter has been listed for further hearing tomorrow.
The matter was mentioned before a bench comprising Chief Justice Devendra Kumar Upadhyaya and Justice Tejas Karia. The PIL was filed by activist-lawyer Rakesh Kumar Saini, who has sought the court’s intervention to ensure measures are taken to protect Wangchuk’s health and life during the protest.
The court issued notices to both the Centre and the Delhi government, seeking their responses to the petition.
CJP announces mass hunger strike on 16 July
The Cockroach Janta Party announced a one-day mass hunger strike on 16 July in solidarity with Wangchuk and the ‘students of this country’.
Why is Wangchuk fasting?
CJP founder Abhijit Dipke announced the Jantar Mantar protest on 6 June.
Wangchuk backed it on X on 2 June, calling CJP members "patriots", and joined the protest on 6 June.
CJP resumed the protest on 20 June. Wangchuk raised two demands:
Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation.
Sixth Schedule status and full statehood for Ladakh.
He gave the Centre until 27 June to respond, saying the fast would end if even one demand was accepted.
After no response, he began an indefinite hunger strike with six AISA members on 28 June.
However, on 13 June, Wangchuk told journalists:
He was at Jantar Mantar over the education issue.
Talks with the Centre on Ladakh were progressing, with broad agreement reached.
The remaining issues could be resolved during the Monsoon Session if the government wished.
Has Sonam Wangchuk fasted before?
Yes. Wangchuk has repeatedly used hunger strikes and marches to press Ladakh's demands.
26 January 2023: Tried to begin a five-day fast at Khardung La (18,000 ft), demanding full statehood. He was placed under house arrest.
18-26 June 2023: Observed an eight-day hunger strike at NDS Stadium, Leh.
September 2024: Led the nearly 1,000-km 'Delhi Chalo Padyatra' from Leh with around 130 supporters. He was detained by Delhi Police at Singhu Border on 30 September.
5-21 October 2024: Began another hunger strike at Ladakh Bhawan, Delhi. He ended it after the Home Ministry assured talks in December.
10 September 2025: Started a 35-day hunger strike with Leh Apex Body (LAB) members.
24 September 2025: Violent protests broke out in Leh. The BJP office was set on fire and four protesters died in police firing.
25 September 2025: Arrested under the National Security Act (NSA) on charges of inciting violence and lodged in Jodhpur Jail.
14 March 2026: Released after 170 days in jail.
